Statement II: The d orbitals in Ga are completely filled.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I: The decrease in first ionization enthalpy from B to Al is much larger than that from Al to Ga.

Statement II: The d orbitals in Ga are completely filled.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
Both the statements I and II are correct
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
Both the statements I and II are incorrect
solution:
First ionization enthalpy (IE1) of B, Al, Ga are 801, 577 and 579 kJ/mol respectively. 

As decrease in first ionization enthalpy is much larger from B to Al. ∆IE1 of B and Al is greater than ∆IE1 of Al and Ga. So, the statement I is correct.

This is due to completely filled d-orbitals in Ga and d-electron have low screening effect to compensate the increase in nuclear charge. So, The statement II is also correct.

Hence, the correct option is B.

q:
The set of correct statements is:

(i) Manganese exhibits +7 oxidation state in its oxide.

(ii) Ruthenium and Osmium exhibit +8 oxidation in their oxides.

(iii) Sc shows +4 oxidation state which is oxidizing in nature.

(iv) Cr shows oxidising nature in +6 oxidation state.
question:
The set of correct statements is:

(i) Manganese exhibits +7 oxidation state in its oxide.

(ii) Ruthenium and Osmium exhibit +8 oxidation in their oxides.

(iii) Sc shows +4 oxidation state which is oxidizing in nature.

(iv) Cr shows oxidising nature in +6 oxidation state.
solution:
(i) The oxidation state of Mn in oxide (Mn2O7) is:

Let us consider Mn as x.

⇒ 2x + 7(-2) = 0

⇒ 2x + (-14) = 0

⇒ 2x -14 = 0

⇒ 2x = 14

⇒x = 7

(ii) The oxidation state of Ruthenium in oxide (RuO4) is:

Consider Ru as x.

⇒ x + 4(-2) = 0

⇒ x - 8 =0

⇒ x = 8

The oxidation state of Osmium in oxide (OsO4) is:

Consider Os as x.

⇒ x + 4(-2) = 0

⇒ x - 8 =0

⇒ x =  8  

Hence, Ruthenium and Osmium has +7 oxidation state in its oxide.

(iii) Sc has ns2 (n-1)d1 electronic configuration.

It utilizes two electrons from its ns sub-shell then its oxidation state = +2. When it utilizes both the electrons then its oxidation state = +3.

(iv) In the modern periodic table, chromium metal is present in group 6. It is a transition metal and belongs to the first transition series. The atomic number of chromium is 24. Its electronic configuration is [Ar] 3d5 4s1. 

It has five electrons in the 3d sub-shell and one electron in the 4s sub-shell. Thus, chromium metal has six valence electrons. Chromium can lose some or all of its valence electrons to form ions with different oxidation states. Thus, chromium shows the oxidation states of +1, +2, +3, +4, +5, and +6 respectively.
The most common oxidation states of chromium are +2, +3, and +6.

So, the statement (i), (ii) and (iv) are correct.

Zns+Sn2+aq⇌Zn2+aq+Sns

question: The concentration of dissolved Oxygen in water for growth of fish should be more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> and Biochemical Oxygen Demand in clean water should be less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> in ppm are, respectively.
solution: The concentration of dissolved oxygen in water is very important for aquatic life. If the concentration of dissolved oxygen of water is below 6 ppm, the growth of fish gets inhibited. The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD). The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically. Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

The indicator ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>’ forms blue colored complex with compound ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>’ present in the solution. The indicator ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math>’ and compound ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>’ respectively are
solution:
The reaction between iodide ions and hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) occurs in the acidic medium and can be represented in the following manner:

2I- (aq) + H2O2 (l) + 2H+ (aq) → I2 (g) + 2H2O (l)

In this reaction, hydrogen peroxide oxidises iodide ions (I-) to molecular iodine. If a calculated amount of sodium thiosulphate is added in the presence of starch solution as an indicator to the above reaction mixture, the liberated iodine reacts with thiosulphate ions as fast as it is formed and is reduced back to iodide ions till all the thiosulphate ions are oxidised to tetrathionate ions.

I2 (g) + 2S2O32- (aq) → S4O62- (aq) + 2I- (aq)

After the complete consumption of thiosulphate ions, the concentration of iodine liberated in the reaction of hydrogen peroxide with iodide ions increases rapidly to a point where iodine forms intense blue complex with starch.

A. Elastomeric polymer I. Urea formaldehyde resin
B. Fibre polymer II. Polystyrene
C. Thermosetting polymer III. Polyester
D. Thermoplastic polymer IV. Neoprene

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match List-I and List-II.

List-I List-II
A. Elastomeric polymer I. Urea formaldehyde resin
B. Fibre polymer II. Polystyrene
C. Thermosetting polymer III. Polyester
D. Thermoplastic polymer IV. Neoprene

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
(A) Rubber is a natural polymer and possesses elastic properties. It is also termed as elastomeric polymer. Neoprene is a family of synthetic rubbers that are produced by the polymerization of chloroprene. So, the correct matching of elastomeric polymer is neoprene.

(B) Polyesters are the polycondensation products of dicarboxylic acids and diols. Dacron or terylene is the best-known example of polyester. Dacron fibre is crease-resistant and is used in blending with cotton and wool fibres and also as glass reinforcing materials in safety helmets, etc. So, the correct matching of fibre polymer is polyester.

(C) Thermosetting polymer which cannot be reused or remoulded. Urea formaldehyde resin and phenol-formaldehyde resins are the thermosetting polymers.

(D) Thermoplastics can soften upon heating and return to their original form on cooling. They are easily moulded and extruded into films, fibres and packaging. Polystyrene is an example of a thermoplastic polymer.

So the correct matching is A-IV, B-III, C-I, D-II.

q:
Match List-I and List-II

List-I List-II
A. Osmosis I. Solvent molecules pass through semi permeable membrane towards solvent side.
B. Reverse osmosis II. Movement of charged colloidal particles under the influence of applied electric potential towards oppositely charged electrodes
C. Electro osmosis III. Solvent molecules pass through semi permeable membrane towards solution side
D. Electrophoresis IV. Dispersion medium moves in an electric field.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match List-I and List-II

List-I List-II
A. Osmosis I. Solvent molecules pass through semi permeable membrane towards solvent side.
B. Reverse osmosis II. Movement of charged colloidal particles under the influence of applied electric potential towards oppositely charged electrodes
C. Electro osmosis III. Solvent molecules pass through semi permeable membrane towards solution side
D. Electrophoresis IV. Dispersion medium moves in an electric field.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
(A) Osmosis: It is a process by which the molecules of a solvent pass from a solution of low concentration to a solution of high concentration through a semi-permeable membrane.

(B) Reverse osmosis: The direction of osmosis can be reversed if a pressure larger than the osmotic pressure is applied to the solution side. Now the pure solvent flows out of the solution through the semi-permeable membrane. This phenomenon is called reverse osmosis.

(C) Electroosmosis: When electrophoresis, i.e., movement of particles is prevented by some suitable means, it is observed that the dispersion medium begins to move in an electric field. This phenomenon is termed electroosmosis.

(D) Electrophoresis: When an electric potential is applied across two platinum electrodes dipping in a colloidal solution, the colloidal particles move towards one or the other electrode. The movement of colloidal particles under an applied electric potential is called electrophoresis.

So the correct matching is A-III, B-I, C-IV, D-II.

question: If the equation of the normal to the curve y=x-ax+bx-2 at the point 1,-3 is x-4y=13 then the value of a+b is equal to ______
solution:
Given curve is

y=x-ax+bx-2   ...1

At point 1,-3, we have

-3=1-a1+b1-2

⇒31+b=1-a

⇒1-a=3+3b

Differentiating 1 both sides w.r.t. x, we get

dydx=(x+b)(x-2)×(1)-(x-a)(2x+b-2)(x+b)2(x-2)2

At (1,-3) slope of normal is 14, hence

dxdy=-14

⇒dydx1,-3=-4,

⇒-4=(1+b)(-1)-(1-a)b(1+b)2(-1)2

⇒-4=(1+b)(-1)-3(b+1)b(1+b)2

⇒-4=-1-3b1+b

∵b≠-1

⇒4+4b=1+3b

⇒b=-3

So, a=7

Hence, a+b=4

Tα,β=0

question:
Let α=8-14i,  A=z∈ℂ:αz-α¯z¯z2-(z¯)2-112i=1 and B=z∈ℂ:z+3i=4

Then, ∑z∈A∩B(Rez-Imz)   is equal to  ________
solution:
Given:

A=z∈ℂ:αz-α¯z¯z2-(z¯)2-112i=1

B=z∈ℂ:z+3i=4

α=8-14i

Let

z=x+iy

So,

αz=8-14ix+iy

⇒αz=(8x+14y)+i(-14x+8y)

Also,

z+z¯=2x and z-z¯=2iy

Set A:

αz-α¯z¯z2-(z¯)2-112i=1

⇒2i-14x+8yz+z¯z-z¯-112i=1

⇒2i-14x+8y4ixy-112i=1

 ⇒-14x+8y2xy-56=1

⇒xy+7x-4y-28=0

⇒x-4y+7=0

⇒x=4 or y=-7

Set B:

z+3i=4

⇒x+iy+3=4

⇒x2+y+32=16

When x=4⇒y=-3

When y=-7⇒x=0

∴A∩B=4-3i,0-7i

So, ∑z∈A∩BRez-Imz=4-(-3)+(0-(-7))=14

solution:
Given functions are:

By integrating 1, we get
f'x=g'x+3x2+C

At x=1

f'1=g'1+3+C

⇒9=3+3+C⇒C=3

∴  f'x=g'x+3x2+3

Again by integrating, we get

fx=gx+x3+3x+D

At x=2, we get

f2=g2+8+32+D

⇒12=4+8+6+D⇒D=-6

So,

fx=gx+x3+3x-6

Option A:

At x=-2,

⇒g-2-f-2=20  

So, this option is true.

Option B

If -1<x<2

Let hx=fx-gx=x3+3x-6

⇒h'x=3x2+3

⇒h'x>0 for all values of x.

So, h-1<hx<h2

⇒-10<hx<8

⇒hx<10     

So, this option is NOT true.

Option C

h'x=f'x-g'x=3x2+3

If h'x<6⇒3x2+3<6

⇒3x2+3<6 and -6<3x2+3

⇒x2<1 and x2>-3always true

⇒-1<x<1        

So, If x∈-1,1 then f'x-g'x<6

So, this option is true.

Option D

fx-gx=0

⇒x3+3x-6=0

hx=x3+3x-6

Here, h1=-ve and h32=+ve

So, there exists x0∈1,32 such that fx0=gx0 

So, this option is true.

solution:
Lets arrange the letters of OUGHT in alphabetical order.

G,H,O,T,U

Number of words starting with G

=4!=24

Number of words starting with H

=4!=24

Number of words starting with  O

=4!=24

Number of words starting with TG

3!=6!

Number of words starting with TH

=3!=6

Number of words starting with TOG

=2!=2

Number of words starting with TOH

Next word is TOUGH.

=24+24+24+6+6+2+2+1=89

Four-digit numbers are 1000,1001,...,9999

Total number of four digit numbers are 9000.

Now,

54=2×33

So, for getting gcd with 2, we must have numbers multiple of 2 but not of 3.

Multiple of 2 are 1000,1002,.....,9998

Let total numbers be m, then

9998=1000+m-12

⇒m=4500

Total numbers are 4500.

Multiple of 6 are 1002,1008....,9996

Let total numbers be n, then

9996=1002+n-16

⇒n=1500

So, required number is =4500-1500=3000

q: The number of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> digit numbers, that are divisible by either <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> or <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> but not divisible by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>48</mn></math> , is
question: The number of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> digit numbers, that are divisible by either <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> or <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math> but not divisible by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>48</mn></math> , is
solution:
We know that,

Total number of three digit number will be 999-99=900,

Now,

Number of 3- digit number which are divisible by 3 will be 9003=300,

Number of 3- digit number which are divisible by 4 will be 9004=225,

Number of 3- digit number which are divisible by 3 & 4 both will be 90012=75,

So , number of 3- digit number which are divisible by 3 or 4 will be 300+225-75=450,

Now, number of 3- digit number which are divisible by 48 is 90048=18

So, number of 3- digit number which are divisible by 3 or 4 but not 48 will be 

=450-18=432

question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> be a relation defined on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">ℕ</mi></math> as a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> b is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>b</mi></math> is a multiple of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">ℕ</mi></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is
solution:
Given:

R=a,b:2a+3b is divisible by 5 for a,b∈N

Reflexive:

2a+3a=5a is always divisible by 5.

So, a,a∈R

Symmetric:

Let a,b∈R i.e., 2a+3b=5λ, where λ∈Z+.

Now,

5a+5b=multiple of 5

⇒2a+3b+2b+3a=multiple of 5

⇒5λ+2b+3a=multiple of 5

⇒2b+3a=multiple of 5-5λ

So, 2b+3a is divisible by 5

Transitive:

Let a,b, b,c∈R, then

2a+3b=5λ1; λ1∈Z+

2b+3c=5λ2; λ2∈Z+

So,

2a+5b+3c=5λ1+λ2

⇒2a+3c=5λ1+λ2-b

Hence, 2a+3c is divisible by 5, so a,c∈R

Hence, R is an equivalence relation.

(C) inversely proportional to the potential gradient of the wire

(D) inversely proportional to the length of the potentiometer wire

question:
With the help of potentiometer, we can determine the value of emf of a given cell. The sensitivity of the potentiometer is

(A) directly proportional to the length of the potentiometer wire

(B) directly proportional to the potential gradient of the wire

(C) inversely proportional to the potential gradient of the wire

(D) inversely proportional to the length of the potentiometer wire

Choose the correct option for the above statements:
solution:
The basic principle of the potentiometer is that the potential drop across any section of the wire will be directly proportional to the length of the wire, provided the wire is of the uniform cross-sectional area and a uniform current flows through the wire.

Therefore, sensitivity of potentiometer wire is inversely proportional to potential gradient.

Sensitivity∝1Vl∝lV

question:

When <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> are connected in parallel and introduced into the left gap, a resistance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is introduced into the right-gap to get null point at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> from left end. question: When two resistance <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> connected in series and introduced into the left gap of a meter bridge and a resistance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is introduced into the right gap, a null point is found at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> from left side. When <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> are connected in parallel and introduced into the left gap, a resistance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi></math> is introduced into the right-gap to get null point at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> from left end. The product of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo> </mo><msub><mi>R</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">Ω</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math>
solution:
A meter bridge consists of a wire of length 1 m and of uniform cross-sectional area stretched taut and clamped between two thick metallic strips bent at right angles with two gaps across which resistors are to be connected. It works on the principle of the Wheatstone bridge. Therefore, we can write

R1+R210=6040⇒R1+R2=15 Ω

Then for the second case, we can write 

R1R2R1+R2×3=4060⇒R1R2=30 Ω2

q: The time period of a satellite of earth is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>24</mn></math> hours. If the separation between the earth and the satellite is decreased to one fourth of the previous value, then its new time period will become.
question: The time period of a satellite of earth is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>24</mn></math> hours. If the separation between the earth and the satellite is decreased to one fourth of the previous value, then its new time period will become.
solution:
From Kepler's law of periods, we know

T2∝R3

⇒T12T22=R13R23⇒T1T22=RR43

⇒T12T22=64

∴T2=248=3 hours

q: At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi>K</mi></math>, the rms speed of oxygen molecules is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mfrac><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow><mi>α</mi></mfrac></msqrt></math> times to that of its average speed in the gas. Then, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> will be (use <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>22</mn><mn>7</mn></mfrac></math>)
question: At <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>300</mn><mo> </mo><mi>K</mi></math>, the rms speed of oxygen molecules is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mfrac><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn></mrow><mi>α</mi></mfrac></msqrt></math> times to that of its average speed in the gas. Then, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> will be (use <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>22</mn><mn>7</mn></mfrac></math>)
solution:
RMS speed is given by, vrms=3RTM and average speed is given by, vavg=8πRTM.

As per question,

vrms=α+5αvavg

⇒3RTM=α+5α8πRTM

⇒3=α+5α×8227

⇒α=28

q: The time taken by an object to slide down <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> rough inclined plane is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> times as it takes to slide down a perfectly smooth <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> incline plane. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the object and the incline plane is:
question: The time taken by an object to slide down <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> rough inclined plane is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> times as it takes to slide down a perfectly smooth <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> incline plane. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the object and the incline plane is:
solution:
Let angle of inclination is θ=45°.

Then, acceleration along the incline in case of smooth incline will be, a1=g sinθ=g2.

In case of rough incline, kinetic friction will act against the direction of motion. Normal force acting on the object will be mgcosθ. Therefore, acceleration along the incline in case of rough incline will be,

a2=g sinθ-μg cosθ=g2-μg2.

Now as per question,

t2=nt1

and as the displacement covered is the same in both case, 0+12a1t12=0+12a2t22

⇒g2t12=g2-μg2n2t12

⇒μ=1-1n2

q: Force acts for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> on a body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math>, starting from rest, after which the force ceases and then body describes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> in the next <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>. The value of force will be :
question: Force acts for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> on a body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math>, starting from rest, after which the force ceases and then body describes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> in the next <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math>. The value of force will be :
solution:
For the second case when body is moving with constant velocity, we can write

50 = V × 10

⇒V=5 m s-1

Now this velocity if the final velocity for the first case, therefore

V = 0+a×20

⇒5=a×20

⇒a=14m s-2

Hence, required force will be

F=ma=20×14= 5 N

q: The electric current in a circular coil of four turns produces a magnetic induction <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>32</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> at its centre. The coil is unwound and is rewound into a circular coil of single turn, the magnetic induction at the centre of the coil by the same current will be :
question: The electric current in a circular coil of four turns produces a magnetic induction <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>32</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> at its centre. The coil is unwound and is rewound into a circular coil of single turn, the magnetic induction at the centre of the coil by the same current will be :
solution:
Magnetic field at the centre of the coil is given by, B=μ0i2R.

Now in the first case:

B=4μ0i2R

and for the second case:

B'=μ0i2R'.

As the length of the wire will remain same, therefore

2πR'=42πR⇒R'=4R

Hence, required ratio will be

B'B=R4R'=116

⇒B'=2 T
